pattern: Enumerating the distinct subsets of a multiset means sorting, then at each value choosing how many copies to include while skipping the branches that would re-create an already-formed subset.
hints: Duplicate values let two different choices produce the same subset, so the enumeration must be ordered to avoid repeats.
Sorting groups equal values together, after which each subset is determined by how many copies of each distinct value it takes.
Either iterate per distinct value over taking zero up to all of its copies, or backtrack adding elements and skip a duplicate at the same depth when its predecessor was not chosen in this branch.

edge_inputs.description: one duplicated value collapses repeated subsets
all distinct behaves like plain subsets

stress.description: short arrays from a tiny value pool so duplicates dominate
stress.note: small pool maximizes duplicate suppression while keeping the subset count bounded
